title: cerastium campanulatum subsp. palustre
intro: Cerastium campanulatum subsp. palustre, also known as marsh chickweed or marsh mouse-ear, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cerastium campanulatum subsp. palustre include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, and it is often used in folk medicine to promote wound healing and support immune function.
